user_query:
Read input/company_policy.yaml and input/data_sources.json (and, if helpful, input/personas.md). Then produce a complete, company-ready Social Scraping Implementation Playbook and supporting artifacts. Use our internal social scraping reference documentation as your source of truth, especially sections named: intro, quickstart, patterns, debugging, performance, security, migration, and cheatsheet. Deliver the following files:

1) output/playbook.md — A comprehensive playbook tailored to our policy and data sources with, at minimum, these top-level sections: Overview, Quickstart, Common Patterns, Debugging, Performance, Security, Migration, Cheatsheet, and References. In Security, align to the company policy (robots.txt, rate limiting, user agent, prohibited actions, data retention, PII handling) and call out concrete controls. In Debugging and Performance, include practical workflows and tips. In References, explicitly list the doc sections you consulted by name: intro, quickstart, patterns, debugging, performance, security, migration, cheatsheet.

2) output/threat_model.json — A STRIDE-based threat model for the scraping pipeline implied by the inputs. Include: assets (list), data_flows (list), and threats (list). Each threat must have: id, category (one of Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege), description, affected_components, mitigations, and dread with fields damage, reproducibility, exploitability, affected_users, discoverability, and score where score equals the sum of those five 0–10 integers. Include methodology with values ["STRIDE", "DREAD"]. Cover all six STRIDE categories at least once.

3) output/sources_matrix.csv — A CSV with columns: source, allowed, reason. Enumerate every source from input/data_sources.json and classify allowed as true/false based on company policy and the source’s attributes (e.g., robots.txt stance, requires_login, terms_scraping_prohibited). Provide a concise reason for each decision.

4) output/checklist.md — A pre-launch operational checklist with at least 8 items that include: logging, error handling, rate limiting, input validation, secure storage, robots.txt, user-agent, backoff/jitter. Keep it actionable.

Ensure all files reflect constraints in input/company_policy.yaml and are internally consistent with each other. Do not assume network access; keep everything self-contained in text. Write only to the output/ paths specified above.
